div1.entryFree:
Startle, 1) intr. to move in a sudden alarm; to be frighted or shocked: patience herself would s. at this letter, As IV, 3, 13. my horse . . . --d, when he looked upon the Tower, R3 III, 4, 87 (Ff started). what fear is this which --s in our ears? Rom. V, 3, 194.
2) tr. to alarm, to rouse, to shock: --s and frights consideration, John IV, 2, 25. --s mine eyes, V, 2, 51. H8 III, 2, 294. Per. V, 1, 147.
